meta.channelTheme: Continental motorcycle documentary — Suzuki DR650, verifiable geography, field-craft technique, zero travel-bro filler.
meta.tone: Treat viewers as intelligent adults. Sound like a disciplined field journal: what to look for, how to film it, what can go wrong, what the maps actually say. Excitement comes from accuracy and stakes—not from exclamation marks.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ The border hack nobody repeats: rehearse a 15-second truth before you stop—home, tonight’s stop, trip purpose, approximate value of gear. Hesitation reads as inconsistency, not humility.
2️⃣ Documents in one sleeve, in order: passport, vehicle registration, insurance. Officers watch hands. Fumbling suggests first crossing or hidden nerves.
3️⃣ Watch the vehicle ahead: engine-on vs engine-off rules differ by booth and shift. Match behaviour; don’t restart until waved—noise signals anxiety.
4️⃣ Secondary inspection is rare for clean paperwork—usually agriculture, cash thresholds, or inconsistent story. Know every organic item in your kit (fruit, jerky, soil on boots).
5️⃣ First hour US-side: confirm phone data actually roams before you film. Dead SIM 40 km in is a production failure, not a ‘story beat.’
6️⃣ Finger Lakes are narrow north–south trenches—post-sunset inversions and fog banks are routine. Arrive with real daylight for unload, tent, and hazard scan—not hero shots in the dark.
1.hook: You are not ‘living your best life.’ You are executing a controlled international port-of-entry on two wheels—with cameras rolling.
1.storyArc: Act I — departure and approach geometry (time to port, fuel, paperwork). Act II — primary inspection as procedure, not drama. Act III — first American miles: verify connectivity, tire temps, mental reset. Denouement — host arrival with enough light to read the ground you’ll sleep on.
talkingPoints: VO: contrast CBP’s job (risk triage) with your job (clean narrative + clean paperwork).
On-camera: show document sleeve and pannier map—teach, don’t perform surprise.
Explain glacial Finger Lakes as trenches—why weather stacks differently than flat Ontario.
Close with one honest fear you had at the booth—specific, not performative.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Thruway tolling without a transponder: know your plate class, keep a payment method that works at every plaza—some lanes are cashless only.
2️⃣ Density gradient: film exposure change (shoulder width, merge geometry, sight lines) as quantitative, not ‘vibes.’
3️⃣ Long Island sound-masking: ocean and traffic hide tire noise—check pressures at a full stop; you won’t hear a slow leak.
4️⃣ Hamptons land use: many shoulders are soft or no-parking—scouting a legal pullout beats a viral ticket.
5️⃣ Heat shimmer on I-495 is real refraction—long lenses exaggerate; wide establishes truth.
6️⃣ End-of-day: if hosted, record permission boundaries for interior audio—documentary ethics on day two already matter.
2.hook: Today isn’t scenery. It’s a lesson in how American road engineering changes when money and population density replace farmland.
2.storyArc: Open on a quiet lake metric (temperature, wind, dew point). Transition to lane mathematics and toll infrastructure. Land in a social environment where your bike is an anomaly—film the contrast as anthropology, not insecurity.
talkingPoints: Explain cashless toll capture for foreign plates—what actually hits your bill and when.
VO on Long Island’s west-to-east income gradient—one road, two economies.
Why DR650 ergonomics matter in stop-and-go parkway merges.
Tease tomorrow: monument sight-lines and I-95 throughput physics.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ DC on a bike: you are low-profile in a city designed for ceremonial vistas—plan sightlines; U-turn rules and parking enforcement are unforgiving.
2️⃣ Heat island + humidity: carburetion and rider hydration scale together—log fluid intake like you log fuel.
3️⃣ I-95 is throughput engineering—film merge physics and following distance, not just ‘traffic bad.’
4️⃣ Shenandoah ascent: brake fade on a loaded bike is cumulative—downshift before the smell starts.
5️⃣ Blue Ridge air mass: afternoon convection builds fast—check radar at fuel stops, not at the overlook.
6️⃣ End card: name three concrete differences between coastal plain riding and ridge-and-valley Appalachia—teach the map.
3.hook: Three physiographic provinces, one day—if you narrate only emotion, you waste the geography.
3.storyArc: Structure as a transect: coastal plain → Piedmont → Blue Ridge foreland. Use map overlays sparingly but precisely. DC sequence is tight B-roll + VO; release into mountain air mass and cooling rates.
talkingPoints: VO: DC’s L’Enfant sightlines as design for power—how a rider experiences width and vista differently than a pedestrian.
Data beat: I-95 AADT context—why mirrors and escape paths matter.
Shenandoah: orographic lift in plain language.
Luray Caverns as karst—tie to regional hydrology if you visit.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Luray → Roanoke: slab vs scenic is a schedule decision—film the Maps tradeoff, not just the pretty valley.
2️⃣ Jefferson NF dispersed: MVUM screenshot on camera—legal tire placement beats Instagram aesthetics.
3️⃣ Ridge-and-valley weather: orographic clouds can form while sun shines in town—show both skies.
4️⃣ Deer math: dusk on two-lane US routes—high beam etiquette and following distance.
5️⃣ Roanoke resupply: grocery run as B-roll—hydration planning is documentary content.
6️⃣ NF camp audio: creek vs road—use as transition bed before VO.
4.hook: You leave Shenandoah country for the chessboard ridges—same Appalachia, different mileage math.
4.storyArc: Morning pack from Luray. Midday: choose corridor with honest ETA. Evening: MVUM-legal camp — bear hang, water discipline, fatigue check.
talkingPoints: Ridge vs valley wind — flags, grass, or stream riffles as evidence.
Jefferson NF as administrative merge with GW — one MVUM system.
Tomorrow: BRP as full-day commitment, not a detour.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Blue Ridge Parkway: 45 mph life—fuel anxiety is honest B-roll; show odometer to next town.
2️⃣ Drag Google route off I-81 — screen record if Maps insists on interstate; teaches audience your method.
3️⃣ Mabry Mill / Rocky Knob: tourist stops block lanes — patience is content.
4️⃣ Boone approach: college-town fuel stops — elevation change vs carb jetting if relevant.
5️⃣ Bristol state line: one frame with both state signs if legal/safe — jurisdiction story without law lecture.
6️⃣ End card: miles on BRP vs miles saved — numbers beat vibes.
5.hook: This isn’t a transit day. It’s a 45 mph thesis on American road design.
5.storyArc: Act I: clean merge onto BRP. Act II: milepost discipline and fuel gaps. Act III: exit to Bristol — twin cities, two states, one afternoon.
talkingPoints: Why the BRP bans trucks — engineering and sightlines, not elitism.
CCC labor history — one minute max, then show pavement.
Boone vs coal country — don’t flatten Appalachia.
Tomorrow: tourism density before the Dragon.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Bristol → Smokies gateway: white balance swings forest shade to tourist-strip neon — lock profile or commit to grade time.
2️⃣ GSMNP reservation screenshot on camera if entering — proof beats argument.
3️⃣ Tail of the Dragon: segment into thirds mentally — fatigue causes offs, not ‘lack of skill.’
4️⃣ Tree of Shame: respectful frame — trauma archaeology, not meme fodder.
5️⃣ Deals Gap fuel: show receipt or pump — documentary credibility.
6️⃣ Cherokee NF camp: bear hang on camera — Leave No Trace as plot beat.
6.hook: Tourism density spikes before the curves — then US-129 invoices ego in 318 installments.
6.storyArc: Transit to Smokies overlook optional. Dragon: skill-not-speed, pullouts honest. Exit to Kingston: leg fatigue as data, not drama.
talkingPoints: Smokies haze — VOC chemistry once, then show ridge gradient.
THP/NC enforcement — public record citations, not gossip.
Cherokee vs NPS jurisdiction — one sentence, map overlay.
Tomorrow: short west TN miles — recovery as content.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Short leg: film the ‘non-hero’ day choice — audience thinks tours are only epic mileage.
2️⃣ Kingston → Parsons: two-lane west TN — farm equipment and dogs; reduce speed through towns.
3️⃣ Kentucky Lake / river optics: brown water = sediment, not always pollution — say once, correctly.
4️⃣ Tick/chigger belt: permethrin vs DEET tradeoff — practical without fear-mongering.
5️⃣ Laundry or chain lube B-roll — maintenance days are watchable if structured.
6️⃣ Map screenshot: short distance truth — credibility with map nerds.
7.hook: Not every day is epic mileage — some days buy admin and sleep-debt recovery.
7.storyArc: Slow morning. West TN two-lane as recovery spin. Parsons arrival — resupply and logistics before the Mississippi crossing tomorrow.
talkingPoints: TVA lake geography — one fact, neutral on politics.
West vs east Tennessee economy — same state, different labor history.
Tomorrow: Little Rock and the river — history beat returns.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Home-stop maintenance: photograph chain slack and sprocket hooks before adjustment—continuity for viewers tracking wear.
2️⃣ Torque critical fasteners cold—post-ride metal reads wrong on a click wrench.
3️⃣ Stockyards cattle drives are timed events—arrive 20 minutes early, establish camera, respect working animals.
4️⃣ Texas heat on black asphalt: check tire pressure cold each morning; show gauge on camera.
5️⃣ Repack flat lay: label what changed and why—viewers steal packing lists, not vibes.
6️⃣ Emotional beat: name one component you’ll replace before the west—honest wear data beats brand loyalty.
8.hook: The trip doesn’t pause at a garage. It enters a different production phase—inspection, torque, and honest wear.
8.storyArc: Morning: arrival geometry. Midday: maintenance as ASMR + instruction (oil, chain, fasteners). Afternoon: cultural texture (Stockyards) as contrast to wrench time. Evening: repack with annotations.
talkingPoints: Republic of Texas history—one fact, cleanly tied to road naming.
Fort Worth vs Dallas identity—economics, not stereotypes.
Why a mid-trip service matters for suspension and steering head bearings on washboard later.
Route 66 preview: myth vs mileage truth.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Panhandle wind: lean angle into gusts is not ‘skill flex’—it’s physics; explain slipstreams when passing semis.
2️⃣ Cadillac Ranch: lead-free paint history matters if kids appear in frame—safety note, not lecture.
3️⃣ Midpoint Adrian: GPS verification on camera—1,139 mi claims need a screen, not a poster trust fall.
4️⃣ I-40 shoulder debris: puncture day—carry patch math and inflation proof.
5️⃣ Altitude still low—heat management for rider and fuel vapor lock on carb bikes.
6️⃣ Golden hour dust: ND filters or accept blown sky—pick a look and commit.
9.hook: Route 66 is decommissioned infrastructure pretending to be nostalgia—film the seams where Interstate killed access.
9.storyArc: Morning: Fort Worth departure metrics. Midday: panhandle relentlessness as time-lapse honesty. Cadillac Ranch: public art + liability. Adrian: midpoint as cartographic fact. Close: wind turbines as land-use story.
talkingPoints: Steinbeck’s Mother Road—literary fact vs road engineering reality.
Grapes of Wrath corridor ecology—dust bowl science in one beat.
Spray paint as participation archaeology—who pays for removal.
Tomorrow: state-line culture shift at the 103rd meridian feeling.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ New Mexico light: low humidity = harsh shadows—fill or bounce, or shoot golden windows only.
2️⃣ Hatch chile is seasonal—if you claim flavor authority, show the menu date.
3️⃣ High desert elevation: jetting and fuel range change—log octane and ping honestly on carb bikes.
4️⃣ Breaking Bad tourism: private property lines—film from public right-of-way.
5️⃣ Petroglyph ethics: no touch, no chalk rubbings—NPS rules are the documentary standard.
6️⃣ Night sky transition: ABQ light dome vs dark east mesa—plan astro shots before moonrise.
10.hook: Enchantment isn’t marketing—it’s refractive index, altitude, and chile genetics. Be specific.
10.storyArc: State line: light color temperature shift. Albuquerque: layered cultures as soundscape + signage. Food: one verifiable chile fact. Science corridor: labs as economy, not jokes. Close: foreshadow elevation gain and cooling.
talkingPoints: Official state question—food chemistry vs tradition.
Albuquerque elevation and engine tuning—DR650 field note.
Trinity site access rules—if mentioned, cite schedule.
San Juan volcanics tomorrow—magma chamber in plain language.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Million Dollar Highway: no guardrail sections mean recovery room is zero—speed to sight distance, not to ego.
2️⃣ Altitude sickness is cumulative—headache + ataxia means stop filming, descend, hydrate.
3️⃣ Brakes cool on straights—pump fade on repeated hairpins is real on heavy bikes.
4️⃣ Silverton oxygen: generators and stoves behave differently—film jetting/starting quirks if present.
5️⃣ Snow beside road in June: reflectance blows exposure—zebra striping in viewfinder.
6️⃣ Camp at elevation: stake frost—record overnight low; viewers in flats need the number.
11.hook: Colorado didn’t install guardrails to insult you—it calculated that false security kills. Understand the engineering argument.
11.storyArc: Climb profile as graph (elevation vs distance). Exposed sections: stable tripod, no lean-out stunts. Town beat: Silverton as working island. Night: stars with stated ISO and exposure—teach, don’t magic.
talkingPoints: San Juan volcanic field—caldera vocabulary without sensationalism.
Silverton railroad endpoint fact—verify timetable if you shoot train.
Ouray bowl geology—why ‘Switzerland’ analogy is structural, not cosmetic.
Descending to desert tomorrow—brake and tire thermal cycle.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Navajo Nation permits: carry receipt on camera person—tribal law supersedes ‘I’m just a tourist.’
2️⃣ Monument Valley telephoto compression: iconic but flattening—wide + long pair establishes truth.
3️⃣ Heat shimmer on US-491: long lenses lie; establish horizon with mid-focal length first.
4️⃣ San Juan River camp: flash-flood awareness in slot-adjacent terrain—weather upstream matters.
5️⃣ Silence recording: 30 seconds ambient-only—documentary gold if levels are clean.
6️⃣ Night: red light discipline if others camp—preserve everyone’s scotopic vision.
12.hook: You’re not in a national park—you’re in a sovereign nation that licenses your presence.
12.storyArc: Descent from alpine to desert via time-lapse color temperature. Four Corners: jurisdiction as geography. Monument Valley: cinema history vs tribal stewardship. River camp: hydrology + quiet.
talkingPoints: John Ford frame vocabulary—and who owns the land in those frames.
US-666 renumber—Congressional trivia with safety outcome.
Navajo government structure—one clear sentence.
Tomorrow: slot canyon timing and tour physics.
